post.description: Converts a long url to a Bitlink. You may see errors returned from this endpoint - "BRANDED_LINK_MONTHLY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED" occurs if you have shortened more links than your account is configured for for the month, and "DNS_CONFIGURATION_ERROR" occurs if you are attempting to shorten links against a custom domain which doesn't have DNS properly configured.

Converts a long url to a Bitlink and sets additional parameters. You may see errors returned from this endpoint - "BRANDED_LINK_MONTHLY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED" occurs if you have shortened more links than your account is configured for for the month, and "DNS_CONFIGURATION_ERROR" occurs if you are attempting to shorten links against a custom domain which doesn't have DNS properly configured.

patch.description: Updates fields in the specified link. To redirect the link (i.e. to update the Long URL), use the long_url parameter. This will always charge an encode limit.

post.description: Returns the short link and long URL for the specified link.

get.description: Returns an array of engagement counts for the specified link. The array is comprised of button click, click and scan counts for each time window, where the window is based on the provided unit.

post.description: Add a keyword (or "custom back-half") to a Bitlink with a Custom Domain (domains must match). This endpoint can also be used for initial redirects to a link.

patch.description: Move a keyword (or custom back-half) to a different Bitlink (domains must match).

patch.description: Bulk update can add or remove tags or archive up to 100 links at a time; The response includes a list of bitlink ids that were updated.

get.summary: Retrieve Top Links and Decoupled QR Codes by Engagements
get.description: Returns the bitlinks and decoupled QR codes in a group with the most engagements over the requested time window, ranked together in descending order by engagement count. Bitlinks and decoupled QR codes are returned in separate lists; `sorted_links` preserves the unified descending-by-count ordering across both, with each entry's id referencing either a bitlink or a QR code guid.

get.summary: Retrieve Sorted Bitlinks for Group
get.description: Returns a list of Bitlinks sorted by group. The list of custom bitlinks has newest entries first.

get.summary: Retrieve Group Shorten Counts

New York, , NY, US, 5

post.summary: Create an inline CSV export for a group
post.description:
"Build a CSV export synchronously and return it inline as a base64 data URI plus metadata.
The export_type field selects the CSV shape: link_engagements_batch (engagement metrics for
explicit bitlinks or a filter), links_list (link metadata roster), or qr_codes_list (QR code
metadata roster). Row caps apply per request: 200 rows when metrics are included
(link_engagements_batch, or include_metrics on a list export); 1000 rows for metadata-only
list exports. When the match set exceeds the cap, truncated is true and only the first
cap rows are returned; row_count reports how many data rows are in the CSV.
"

get.summary: Retrieve Organizations
get.description: Retrieve a list of organizations for the authenticated user. By default, only organizations permitted by the token's allowed_orgs are returned. Use include_all=true to return all organizations regardless of token restrictions. Each organization is annotated with its authentication requirements (require_sso, require_2fa) when configured.

parameters.name: include_all
parameters.description: When set to true, returns all of the user's organizations regardless of the token's allowed_orgs restrictions.

post.description: Create a new QR Code and return its metadata

post.summary: Generate a static QR Code image
post.description:
"Generate a static QR code image without creating or saving a QR code resource. SVG and PNG output are supported via the Accept header
Contact your Account Manager to discuss enabling this feature for your account.

The `render_customizations` field supports basic appearance options. Customizations that require the rasterize API are not supported: `gradient`, `background_gradient`, `frame`, `text`, and `logo`.

The Accept header controls the response format:
- `application/json` (default): Returns a JSON object with the image as a base64-encoded byte string.
- `image/svg+xml`: Returns the raw SVG image.
- `image/png`: Returns the raw PNG image.
"

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: Controls the response format. Use `application/json` (default) to receive a JSON-wrapped base64 image, `image/svg+xml` to receive the raw SVG, or `image/png` to receive the raw PNG.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The image format. Supports `svg` and `png`. Ignored if the Accept header specifies an image type.

get.summary: Retrieve a QR Code image
get.description:
Get the image of a given QR Code. The Accept header controls how this is returned (options are "application/json", "image/svg+xml", and "image/png").
If the Accept header is "application/json" the format query parameter will determine the image format inside of the json (which will be base64 encoded and prefixed with its mime type as would be suitable for a HTML image src tag).
To Return the QR Code image itself, set the Accept header to "image/svg+xml" or "image/png", or remove the Accept header and set the format query parameter to “svg” or “png” (if no format is specified the default will be “svg”).

parameters.name: Accept
parameters.description: The file format for the QR code's image. If the accept header is application/json the image data will be wrapped in a json object, base64 encoded and prefixed with its mime type as would be suitable for a HTML image src tag.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format type of the image. If there is an image format in the header it takes precedence.

parameters.name: decoupled
parameters.description: When true, ranks results by decoupled QR scan count and returns a TopLinksAndQRCodes response with links and QR codes partitioned into separate lists and a unified sorted_engagements ordering.

next.description: a hyperlink to the next set of results.

search_after.description: the identifier of where to begin searching for the next set of results.

size.description: the number of results returned from this query.

QrPagination.description: This object specifies how to query the next set of results.

custom_bitlinks.description: list of previously edited backhalfs for the bitlink - these are in reverse chronological order, with the most recently edited backhalf first
list of previously edited backhalfs for the bitlink - these are in reverse chronological order, with the most recently edited backhalf first

qr_code_ids.description: a list of qr code ids that are associated with this bitlink

expiration_at.description: Optional expiration timestamp for the bitlink (e.g., 2025-01-28T14:30:00+0000)

custom_bitlinks.description: list of previously edited backhalfs for the bitlink - these are in reverse chronological order, with the most recently edited backhalf first

qr_code_ids.description: a list of qr code ids that are associated with this bitlink

expiration_at.description: Optional expiration timestamp for the bitlink (e.g., 2025-01-28T14:30:00+0000)
Optional expiration timestamp for the bitlink (e.g., 2025-01-28T14:30:00+0000). Minimum expiration time is 5 minutes from now and maximum is 1 year.

EngagementSubtotal.description: Engagement-count subtotal for a single bitlink or decoupled QR code. The `id` references either a bitlink (`<domain>/<hash>`) or a QR code guid, and `engagements` is the combined count of bitlink clicks or QR code scans for that entity.

TopLinksAndQrCodes.description: Response shape for top bitlinks and decoupled QR codes in a group, ranked together by engagement count. `links` and `qr_codes` are independent lists (a link and a decoupled QR code are never the same entity); `sorted_engagements` is the unified descending-by-engagement ordering across both, with each entry's id referencing either a bitlink or a QR code guid. Coupled QRCs are listed by their bitlink ID.

TimeUnit.description: the unit of time queried for (minute, hour, day, week, month)

TimeUnitDwm.description: the unit of time queried for (day, week, month)

long_url.description: URL to be shortened. Required when creating new bitlinks. Cannot be used with bitlink_id.

group_guid.description: Always include a specific group and custom domain in your shorten calls.

keyword.description: Custom keyword for creating keyword overrides. If provided, creates an override at domain/keyword

bitlink_id.description: "Existing bitlink ID to add a keyword override to. Format: domain/hash. Cannot be used with long_url"

expiration_at.description: Optional expiration timestamp for the bitlink (e.g., 2025-01-28T14:30:00+0000). Minimum expiration time is 5 minutes from now and maximum is 1 year.

FullShorten.description: Either long_url (for new bitlinks) or bitlink_id (for adding keywords to existing bitlinks) must be provided, but not both.

count.description: the usage count during the time period

unit_start.description: ISO_TIMESTAMP of the beginning of the time period

limit_usage.description: an array of all of the group's consumable feature usage

unit.description: A unit of time (day, week, or month)

start_date.description: Start date (format "2006-01-02") of the historical usage

end_date.description: End date (format "2006-01-02") of the historical usage

historical_limit_usage.description: an array of all of the group's historical feature usage

bitlinks.description:
Fully qualified bitlinks (e.g. "bit.ly/abc"). Used by export_type link_engagements_batch when
the caller already knows the exact links. Mutually exclusive with filter (provide one or the other).

unix_from.description:
Start of the metrics date range (Unix seconds, UTC). Clamped to the caller's tier data window.
For link_engagements_batch, required together with unix_to unless the tier max-history default applies.
For links_list or qr_codes_list, used when include_metrics is true.

unix_to.description: End of the metrics date range (Unix seconds, UTC). See unix_from.

include_metrics.description:
When true on links_list or qr_codes_list, append clicks, scans, and button_clicks columns and apply
the 200-row cap. Requires unix_from and unix_to (or tier-default window behavior where applicable).

created_after.description: Include items created at or after this Unix timestamp (UTC).

created_before.description: Include items created before this Unix timestamp (UTC).

campaign_guid.description: Filter links by campaign GUID. Ignored for qr_codes_list.

GroupExportFilter.description: Search constraints used to resolve links or QR codes for export. Required for links_list and qr_codes_list.

filename.description: Suggested download filename for the CSV.

row_count.description: Number of data rows in the CSV (excluding the header row).

size_bytes.description: Size of the raw CSV bytes before base64 encoding.

data_uri.description: CSV file as a data:text/csv;base64,... URI.

unix_from.description: Metrics window start (Unix seconds) when metrics are included in the export.

unix_to.description: Metrics window end (Unix seconds) when metrics are included in the export.

truncated.description: True when the match set exceeded the per-request row cap and only the first cap rows were exported.

links.description: this is limited to 100 bitlink ids

tags.name: Groups
tags.description: Groups are subdivisions within organizations. Every user will be in at least one group, and every shortened link will be in one group. Most actions on our API will be on behalf of a group. For example, when you shorten a link, it will be on behalf of a user and a group.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Organizations are equivalent to a Bitly account. Every plan limit, group, and user is tied to an organization.

tags.name: User
tags.description: Some subscription plans allow multiple users. Only one user is used for authenticating a call, and these operations apply only to the authenticated user.

tags.name: BSDs
tags.description: Branded short domains (BSDs) are the custom domains used to brand your links. A BSD can be added to a Bitly organization and may be assigned to one or more groups. They can contain up to 32 characters.

tags.name: Bitlinks
tags.description:
Bitlinks are shortened links. They could begin with the bit.ly domain or your own custom branded short domain (BSD).

**Related Operations:**
- [POST /bitlinks](#createFullBitlink) - Create a new bitlink or add a custom keyword to an existing bitlink
- [PATCH /bitlinks/{bitlink}](#updateBitlink) - Redirect an existing bitlink

tags.name: Custom Bitlinks
tags.description: These endpoints are for updating custom Bitlinks only. Custom Bitlinks have both a BSD and a customized back-half (e.g., yourcompany.com/yourcampaign). If a link begins with the bit.ly domain or ends with an auto-generated random string it is not a custom Bitlink.

tags.name: Campaigns
tags.description: Bitly Campaigns are used to build and organize links and metrics for marketing campaigns.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Webhooks are a method of sending information from Bitly to an application outside of Bitly. It's a way to automate actions or updates between two different systems. These endpoints are for configuring your webhooks.

tags.name: Deeplinks
tags.description: Deeplinks refers to links that direct a user to a location inside of a mobile application. Bitly allows accounts to configure varying implementations of Deeplinks.

tags.name: Billing
tags.description: All endpoints under this tag are related to accounts subscribing to the Bitly service.

tags.name: Tiers
tags.description: Tiers references what level subscription a Bitly account is under.

tags.name: SSO
tags.description: SSO is an acronym for Single Sign-On. This relates to a method of authentication used by some Bitly accounts.

tags.name: Invitations
tags.description: Invitations allow new users to be invited to a Bitly organization via email or username. These endpoints handle both creation, retrieval, and deletion of invitations tied to an organization.

tags.name: Bulk Shorten
tags.description: Bulk Shorten references the capability for users to upload a csv of links they would like to shorten and optionally customize.

tags.name: Launchpads
tags.description: A link launchpad is a customizable page that contains a collection of links. These endpoints allow for the customization of the links associated with this site and the appearance of the site itself.

tags.name: Images
tags.description: Images can be uploaded to be use in a Bitly QR code. Images can be uploaded, cropped, re-cropped, and fetched via the API. Each image includes an Image GUID and a public URL hosting the image.

tags.name: QR Codes
tags.description: QR codes can be created and customized with different dot patterns, corner shapes, colors, and more. They can contain bitlinks, links to microsites, or several kinds of static data.

tags.name: AI Assist
tags.description: AI Assist insights and chat functionality for data analysis and link creation.
